chunks.title: God's Power and Man's Inability to Contend
chunks.reason: Job argues that God is too powerful for a man to dispute with Him.

chunks.title: The Futility of Job's Righteousness
chunks.reason: Job describes how even if he were righteous, God would still condemn him.

chunks.title: The Swiftness of Days and Hopeless Purity
chunks.reason: Job laments the speed of his life and the futility of trying to be pure before God.

chunks.title: The Need for a Mediator
chunks.reason: Job concludes that without an arbitrator between him and God, he cannot speak freely.
